codeReview.summary: The deprecation module is disconnected from actual MemoryEntry storage. The feature creates types and tests but is never integrated with the storage layer. All 4 blocking issues from prior reviews are STILL PRESENT.

issues.description: MemoryEntry struct has NO confidence field. The feature requires checking confidence < 0.3 to mark as deprecated, but MemoryEntry (line 196-235) has no confidence field anywhere in its definition. Fields present: id, block_type, label, content, embedding, created_at, updated_at, metadata, repository, language, task_type, last_reinforcement, is_stale, source_episode_id, evidence, provenance_context.

SqliteMemoryStore init_schema() has NO deprecation columns. The CREATE TABLE memory_entries statement (line 182-196) does not include: is_deprecated, superseded_by, deprecation_reason, or deprecated_at columns. DeprecationInfo cannot be persisted to database.

Deprecatable trait is defined at line 130 (fn get_confidence(&self) -> f64) but NEVER IMPLEMENTED for any type. 'grep -r "impl Deprecatable"' returns no matches in entire codebase. The trait is dead code.

check_deprecation() (line 252) and apply_confidence_deprecation() (line 288) functions exist but are NEVER CALLED from anywhere in the codebase outside their own tests. 'grep -r "check_deprecation\|apply_confidence_deprecation"' shows they are only referenced in their own test module and in lib.rs exports - not called by any actual memory store implementation. They are dead code.

summary: ROUND 11 BLOCKING ISSUES NOT FIXED. No new commits to deprecation.rs since round 1. All 4 blocking issues remain: (1) MemoryEntry has no confidence field (2) SqliteMemoryStore schema has no deprecation columns (3) Deprecatable trait is never implemented (4) check_deprecation() and apply_confidence_deprecation() are dead code never called from storage layer. The feature cannot function as specified - memories cannot be marked deprecated because there is no confidence field to check, no way to persist deprecation state, and no integration with memory store. This issue persists unchanged for 11 rounds.
